agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"HTTP ingestion endpoint","to":"internal event pipeline","status":"correct","issue":"Well-structured intake with request ID propagation and structured logging"},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"data warehouse","status":"broken","issue":"Writes are not idempotent. Retries from exponential backoff and at-least-once delivery both produce duplicate records in the warehouse. The retry mechanism actively worsens data integrity under any transient failure."},{"from":"event pipeline","to":"dead-letter queue","status":"broken","issue":"DLQ exists but has no consumer, no alerting on queue depth, and no reprocessing path. Failed events silently accumulate and are permanently lost in practice."},{"from":"circuit breaker","to":"retry logic","status":"risk","issue":"Circuit breaker trips after sustained warehouse failures, but retried-then-succeeded events may already be duplicated. No coordination between circuit breaker state transitions and deduplication."}],"contract_compliance":"The service promises event delivery to the data warehouse but cannot guarantee exactly-once semantics. Any downstream analytics, billing, or decision-making built on this data will operate on inflated counts. This is a data integrity contract violation, not just a cosmetic issue.","data_flow_integrity":"Compromised. The combination of at-least-once delivery, non-idempotent writes, and no deduplication means the warehouse data is unreliable by design, not just under edge cases. Every transient network hiccup or warehouse slowdown inflates event counts."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"adequate","gaps":["No logging or metrics on duplicate event detection (because detection does not exist)","DLQ depth and age-of-oldest-message are not logged or alerted on"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Strong — Prometheus histograms, p99 dashboards, alert rules configured","gaps":["No metric for duplicate event rate or deduplication hit/miss ratio","No metric for DLQ depth, DLQ consumer lag, or DLQ oldest message age","No metric distinguishing first-delivery writes from retry-delivery writes"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Good — OpenTelemetry distributed tracing with request ID propagation","gaps":["Traces should tag retry attempts vs original attempts so duplicate writes are visible in trace queries"]},"debugging_capability":"Good for latency and throughput debugging. Poor for data integrity debugging — an on-call engineer at 3am cannot distinguish a duplicate-inflated spike from a genuine traffic spike without manual warehouse queries."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"Transient data warehouse timeout during write","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Retry fires and succeeds, but original write may also have committed. MUST add idempotency keys (event_id + idempotent upsert/INSERT ON CONFLICT IGNORE) to warehouse writes. This is the critical fix."},{"scenario":"Sustained data warehouse outage","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Circuit breaker correctly trips and events route to DLQ. But DLQ has no consumer — events are lost in practice. Deploy a DLQ consumer with backoff-based reprocessing and alert on queue depth > 0 within 5 minutes."},{"scenario":"ACK lost between warehouse and ingestion service","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"At-least-once delivery guarantees this happens under normal operation. Without idempotent writes, every lost ACK creates a duplicate record. Idempotency key is the only fix."},{"scenario":"DLQ fills up or backing store runs out of space","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No monitoring, no alerting, no consumer. Add queue depth alerts, disk usage alerts, and a retention/eviction policy with dead-letter-dead-letter escalation or operator notification."},{"scenario":"Burst beyond 10K events/sec","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Load tested at 10K sustained, but no documented behavior for bursts above capacity. Add backpressure signaling (HTTP 429 with Retry-After) and shed load gracefully rather than silently dropping or duplicating."}],"retry_config":"Exponential backoff with 3 retries — well configured but dangerous without idempotent writes. The retry mechanism is currently a duplication engine.","timeout_config":"Not explicitly documented — verify warehouse write timeout is set and is shorter than the circuit breaker evaluation window.","circuit_breakers":"Present on warehouse writes — good. Verify half-open state probe behavior and that metrics track state transitions.","graceful_degradation":"Partial. Circuit breaker + DLQ is the right pattern, but the DLQ leg is incomplete without a consumer. Graceful degradation currently means 'silently lose events'."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Blue/green deployment with load balancer traffic switch enables instant rollback. This is well-designed and production-grade.","monitoring_coverage":"Strong for latency and availability. Critical gap for data integrity — no way to detect or alert on duplicate records or DLQ accumulation.","on_call_documentation":"Needs runbook entries for: (1) how to detect and remediate duplicate warehouse records, (2) how to manually drain and reprocess the DLQ, (3) circuit breaker state transition procedures, (4) what to do when DLQ depth alerts fire.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":["DLQ consumer/reprocessor service"],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add idempotency key column and unique constraint to warehouse target table","Add DLQ depth and age alerting rules"],"infra_provisioning":[]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":true,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":true,"details":"Adding a unique constraint or idempotency key index on a high-volume clickstream table requires careful migration planning. On a table ingesting 10K events/sec, adding an index or constraint may lock the table or cause significant write amplification. Use online DDL (e.g., CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY or warehouse-specific equivalent) and schedule during low-traffic window."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Non-idempotent warehouse writes with at-least-once delivery guarantee","reason":"This is not an edge case — it is a guaranteed data corruption path under normal operation. Every transient failure, every lost ACK, every retry produces duplicate records. Downstream analytics will report inflated numbers. If this data feeds billing, recommendations, or business metrics, the impact is material. Must implement idempotent upserts with a unique event_id before shipping.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"Dead-letter queue has no consumer or alerting","reason":"The DLQ is a black hole. Events routed there during warehouse outages are silently lost. This negates the resilience benefit of having a DLQ in the first place. Need a DLQ consumer service and queue-depth alerting before shipping.","routed_to":"Shaq"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No metrics or alerting for duplicate event rate or DLQ health","reason":"On-call engineer cannot distinguish a duplicate-inflated traffic spike from a real one. Cannot detect DLQ accumulation. Add: duplicate_events_total counter, dlq_depth gauge, dlq_oldest_message_age_seconds gauge, and alert rules for each.","routed_to":"Shaq"}],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Idempotency gap is a guaranteed production data integrity issue, not theoretical","DLQ without consumer is verifiably incomplete","Observability and deployment posture are genuinely strong"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact warehouse DDL migration strategy depends on specific warehouse technology (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake, etc.)","Burst behavior beyond 10K/sec is untested — risk level depends on traffic patterns"],"assumptions":["Downstream systems treat warehouse data as source of truth for analytics","Event deduplication is not handled by any downstream ETL or query layer","The warehouse technology supports some form of upsert or unique constraint"]}}
